summary: Decompiled a substantial slice of src/melee/it/items/itbombhei.c, replacing multiple placeholder functions with C for BombHei dropped/thrown/air-entry and motion-state behavior. decomp.dev reported +6020 matched code bytes, +48 matched data bytes, 11 new matches, and 7 improved still-unmatched BombHei functions; the author separately noted several remaining hard functions at roughly 90-96% match. The PR also tightened BombHei item/attribute structs, renamed ItemCommonData::x30 to x30_lifetime across common item users, and made mostly formatting/include cleanup in adjacent item/fighter/stage files.
key_files: src/melee/it/items/itbombhei.c - main decomp work; added GET_ATTRS, math/lb includes, BombHei state/motion implementations, lifetime usage, preserve-bone state transitions, and Vec3 it_803B8600.
src/melee/it/items/itbombhei.static.h - refactored preserve-bone inline helpers to accept Item_80268E5C flags and added itBombhei_UpdateStatePreserveBoneFake.
src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h - corrected BombHei item-var and attribute field types; added itBombHeiAttributes::x20 Vec3.
src/melee/it/item.c, src/melee/it/it_2725.c, src/melee/it/items/itfreeze.c, src/melee/it/items/itgshell.c - updated callers from it_804D6D28->x30 to x30_lifetime.
src/melee/it/items/itlikelike.c - mostly style/include/cast cleanup; relevant because the author copied its GET_ATTRS pattern.
src/melee/it/items/itmatadogas.c - include ordering and formatting around itMatadogasAttributes.
src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.c, src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.h, src/melee/gr/grinishie2.c - line wrapping/formatting only in the shown diff.
what_changed: Filled BombHei placeholders including it_3F14_Logic6_Dropped, it_8027E978, itBombhei_UnkMotion3_Anim, it_8027EE04, itBombhei_UnkMotion2_Anim, itBombhei_UnkMotion2_Coll, it_8027F42C, itBombhei_UnkMotion4_Anim, it_8027F8E0, fn_8027FCA8, fn_8028007C, it_3F14_Logic6_Thrown, itBombhei_UnkMotion10_Anim, fn_80280974, it_80280B60, and it_3F14_Logic6_EnteredAir.

decomp.dev marked 11 new matches: it_8027F8E0, fn_8027FCA8, it_3F14_Logic6_Thrown, it_3F14_Logic6_Dropped, it_80280B60, it_8027E978, it_8027EE04, it_8027F42C, it_3F14_Logic6_EnteredAir, .sdata2, and it_8027D670.

Several BombHei functions remained partial but improved, including fn_80280974, itBombhei_UnkMotion2_Coll, itBombhei_UnkMotion3_Anim, itBombhei_UnkMotion10_Anim, itBombhei_UnkMotion2_Anim, itBombhei_UnkMotion4_Anim, and fn_8028007C.

Changed it_8027D670 to compute facing_dir from &data.prev_pos rather than &data.pos, a small stack/addressing-sensitive matching adjustment after data.pos and data.prev_pos are both initialized from *pos.
Generalized itBombhei_UpdateStatePreserveBone from a fixed ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE helper to a helper taking flags, and replaced multiple near-duplicate helper variants with normal and fake setter forms.
Updated BombHei structs: itBombHei_ItemVars::xDF4 became float, _E04 became f32 xE04, itBombHeiAttributes::xC became float, and itBombHeiAttributes gained x20 Vec3.
Renamed common item lifetime field x30 to x30_lifetime and updated uses in item spawn/lifetime paths and shell/freezer/BombHei code.
Cleaned formatting in itlikelike, itmatadogas, it_27CF, ft_0C31, and grinishie2 without strong evidence of semantic changes there.
smart_moves: Used a preserve-bone wrapper around Item_80268E5C so dynamic bone 0xB translation Y and rotation X survive state changes; the wrapper takes exact flags so ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, ITEM_UNK_0x1, 0x12, 0x1A, and 0x104 cases can share code.
Kept a separate itBombhei_UpdateStatePreserveBoneFake path using fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslateY/fake_HSD_JObjSetRotationX, preserving a matching lever while removing four duplicated helper bodies.

Typed BombHei attributes enough to make velocity and bounds logic readable: attrs->xC drives x40_vel.x, attrs->x20.x/y are used in ABS checks before it_80280DC0, and attrs->x18/x8 feed lifetime/state variables.
Promoted the common item data lifetime guess into the explicit name x30_lifetime and updated all shown users, making later item decomp less dependent on a comment-only field name.
Tracked progress with decomp.dev and easy_funcs.py; the PR clearly separated 100% new matches from still-hard 90-96% functions instead of hiding partial-match status.
decomp_lessons: Item specialAttributes casts can improve readability and expose struct field types, but per-file GET_ATTRS macros are not settled project convention; use them cautiously and be ready to inline/break them for permuter/codegen needs.
For item animation-state transitions, preserving dynamic bone transforms around Item_80268E5C is a reusable pattern; exact setter choice, fake setter choice, and flags can affect matching.
Small source-address choices such as passing &data.prev_pos instead of &data.pos can be enough to finish a near-match even when values are semantically identical at initialization.
When an item-global field is proven by repeated use, renaming it in the shared struct and updating all callers is better than local casts or comments; x30_lifetime is the example here.
Struct field signedness/type matters for matching and meaning: BombHei xDF4 and attribute xC are floats, xE04 is f32, and a trailing Vec3 attribute x20 was needed for bounds checks.
A PR can still be valuable when several large functions are not 100%; document the remaining functions and match percentages so future decomp work knows exactly where to resume.
naming_conventions: BombHei symbols in this file use itBombhei_UnkMotionN_{Anim,Phys,Coll} for unnamed motion callbacks and it_3F14_Logic6_{Dropped,Thrown,EnteredAir} for common item logic callbacks.

Unclear behavior functions stayed address-style or fn-style, such as it_8027E978, it_8027EE04, it_8027F42C, it_8027F8E0, fn_8027FCA8, fn_8028007C, fn_80280974, and it_80280B60; no semantic rename was invented.
Shared item data lifetime is now ItemCommonData::x30_lifetime rather than x30 with a lifetime comment.
BombHei data remains in xDD4_itemVar.bombhei with offset-like members, but field names/types were refined where evidence existed: xDF4 float, xE04 f32, and itBombHeiAttributes::x20 Vec3.
Reviewer mentioned a possible future generic macro shape like GET_ATTRS(ip, itBombHeiAttributes), but this PR used local GET_ATTRS(ip) and did not establish a global convention.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) and PAD_STACK(0x18) in several BombHei functions to satisfy stack layout.
Used fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslateY/fake_HSD_JObjSetRotationX through itBombhei_UpdateStatePreserveBoneFake when normal setters did not match.
Preserved dynamic bone 0xB by reading HSD_JObjGetTranslationY and HSD_JObjGetRotationX before Item_80268E5C, then restoring them after the animation/state update.
Passed exact Item_80268E5C flags through helper calls instead of baking in one flag, allowing matches for ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, ITEM_UNK_0x1, 0x12, 0x1A, and 0x104 call sites.
Kept explicit GET_ITEM reloads, temporaries, and casts in motion/collision code; examples include function-pointer casting for it_8026D8A4(gobj, (void (*)(HSD_GObj*)) it_8027F42C).
Used math.h/M_PI double arithmetic for the BombHei rotation increment in it_8027E978 and lb_800119DC in it_80280B60, adding includes only where needed.
Author noted that the GET_ATTRS macro sometimes had to be broken apart for permuter reasons, such as introducing article-data intermediates.
review_feedback: ribbanya questioned whether the local GET_ATTRS(ip) macro meaningfully fixed stack/codegen and noted that fighters have similar patterns but the project had not defined macros for them.
ribbanya said the team had considered a generic macro form such as GET_ATTRS(ip, itBombHeiAttributes), but had not implemented it.
jurrejelle replied that the pattern was copied from itlikelike, helped readability and some cases without a between-var, but had to be broken in places for permuter/matching.
ribbanya concluded that if the macro cannot be used consistently it should probably be avoided pending further investigation, though using it or not did not matter much for this PR.
No other substantive review objections are present in the slice; the PR was merged shortly after the GET_ATTRS discussion.
follow_up_queries: Continue BombHei partials from the author/decomp.dev lists: fn_80280974, itBombhei_UnkMotion2_Coll, itBombhei_UnkMotion3_Anim, itBombhei_UnkMotion10_Anim, itBombhei_UnkMotion8_Anim, itBombhei_UnkMotion2_Anim, itBombhei_UnkMotion4_Anim, and fn_8028007C.
Investigate whether a project-wide typed attribute helper like GET_ATTRS(ip, Type) is worth adopting, or whether local GET_ATTRS macros should be avoided for consistency.
Audit remaining it_804D6D28->x30 references, if any, after the x30_lifetime rename.
Refine semantic names for BombHei attrs x0/x4/x8/xC/x14/x18/x20 and item vars xDD4/xDE0/xDF0/xE04 once behavior is understood.
Check whether preserve-bone/fake-setter helper patterns in itbombhei.static.h can be reused safely in other item decomp units without harming consistency.
Separate purely formatting cleanup in itlikelike/grinishie2/ft_0C31 from future semantic decomp PRs if those files become review-noisy.
